title: sIfA Tool — Statement of Intellectual Fellowship and Accountability
description:
A browser-based tool for declaring how AI tools contributed to a piece of research, mapped onto the 14 CRediT contributor roles maintained by NISO. Produces a structured table and a single-page visualisation (the "sIfA figure") that can be attached to a publication, report, or institutional disclosure.

sIfA stands for Statement of Intellectual Fellowship and Accountability. Fellowship treats AI tools as named collaborators in the work, not invisible utilities. Accountability keeps responsibility for the published work with the human author, who is making the disclosure deliberately and transparently.

The tool is distributed as a single self-contained HTML file (online and fully offline builds are provided). No install, no server, no build step required for end users.
notes: Bundles React 18, ReactDOM 18 and Babel Standalone 7 in the offline build under their respective MIT licences (see THIRD_PARTY_LICENSES.md). The underlying CRediT taxonomy is a NISO standard, referenced under its own terms; this product is not endorsed by or affiliated with NISO.
